page.content: In the Fiscal Year 2024 report for Toyota Motor, the Vehicle Sales Volume by Model details show that the SUV model sold 20,000 units, generating CHF500M in revenue, capturing a 25% market share, with a growth rate of 10%, an average price of CHF25,000, classified under the Compact segment, with year-over-year units sold at 15,000 and total units revenue at CHF375M. The Sedan model sold 10,000 units, amounting to CHF250M in revenue, holding a 15% market share, a growth rate of 3%, with an average price of CHF25,000.

lines.text: In the Fiscal Year 2024 report for Toyota Motor, the Vehicle Sales Volume by Model details show that the SUV model sold
20,000 units, generating CHF500M in revenue, capturing a 25% market share, with a growth rate of 10%, an average price of
CHF25,000, classified under the Compact segment, with year-over-year units sold at 15,000 and total units revenue at
CHF375M. The Sedan model sold 10,000 units, amounting to CHF250M in revenue, holding a 15% market share, a growth rate
of 3%, with an average price of CHF25,000.

page.content: Total sales reached 45,000 units, yielding CHF1.125B in revenue, representing a 60% market share, and a growth rate of 6%, with an average price of CHF25,000 for Electric at 5,000 units and CHF125M revenue, while the Pickup sold 8,000 units for CHF200M, with an 18% market share and 4% growth rate.

lines.text: Total sales reached 45,000 units, yielding CHF1.125B in revenue, representing a 60% market share, and a growth rate of 6%,
with an average price of CHF25,000 for Electric at 5,000 units and CHF125M revenue, while the Pickup sold 8,000 units for
CHF200M, with an 18% market share and 4% growth rate.

page.content: Manufacturing Plant Utilization reflects Plant A located at 12,000 monthly average capacity with an 85.5% utilization rate, producing 10,200 units with 5.2 defects per 1,000, marked as Active and on schedule for Q22024; Plant B at 15,000 capacity with a 78.3% utilization rate producing 11,745 units with 7.4 defects per 1,000, also Active but needs improvement for Q32024; Plant C at 8,000 capacity showing 90.0% utilization producing 7,200 units with 3.5 defects per 1,000, currently Inactive under maintenance for Q42024; Plant D at 10,000 capacity showing 92.5% utilization producing 9,250 units with 4.0 defects per 1,000, marked Active and performing well. Overall, the combined capacity of Plants A-D totals 50,000 with an 83.3% utilization rate producing 39,295 units with 5.7 defects per 1,000, classified as N/A for overall performance in the Summary Report.
